metadata.title:
SPOKANE UNIONS
TO STAND BY


GOMPERS

pages.raw_text:
Members of Spokane unions who
express themselves in regard to the
attack Speaker Cannon made on
President Gompers last night say
that Cannon is but poorly informed
in regard to the workings If the
American Federation of Labor.
They claim that Gompers Is only
the servant of the unions and that
he has but little authority other
than what he receives from the in
terna tionals which comprise the
federation. 1t is argued from this
that Mr.. Gompers is not setting
himselg up as a car but is only
obeying orders as it is his duty to
do as union man and an officer.
The attempt of Mr.. Cannon to APP
peal to the loyalty of the republi
can wOrkingmen and voters t. vote
the same old machine ticket will
fall upon unheeding ears if local
union men can be believed They
claim that the unions do not ask
for anything that they are not will
ing to leave to a vote of all the
people of the country, hence their
agitation for the initiative and ref
erendum and direct primary. They
claim that Mr.. Cannon, the corpor-
alton newspapers and every inter
est in the land that has fattened
off the public is opposed to union
labor and the reforms they advo-
cate and which they believe VIII
benefit the entire country.


The union men say that they are
willing and anxious to aid Mr..
Gompers in every way and that any
assessments for carrying on the
labor campaign will be cheerfully
met by members of all parties.
